Title: View payment documents
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order with payment exists
Steps:
1. Open order > Documents section
2. Verify payment document is listed
3. Open payment document details

Title: Payment document status changes
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Payment document exists
Steps:
1. Open payment document
2. Change payment status through available transitions
3. Verify each status change

Title: Cancel payment document
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Payment document exists
Steps:
1. Open payment document
2. Click 'Cancel'
3. Confirm cancellation

Title: Payment document details (address and price blocks)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order with payment and shipping
Steps:
1. Open payment document
2. Verify Payment address block with 'Same as shipping address' checkbox
3. Verify Payment price block
4. Verify Transactions block

Title: Payment document with new order
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
New order created
Steps:
1. Create new order
2. Open payment document
3. Verify initial state

Title: Capture authorized payment (manual method)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Payment in status 'Authorized' with manual payment method
Steps:
1. Open order with authorized payment
2. Click 'Capture' on payment document
3. Verify capture result

Title: Capture authorized payment (authorize.net and native) fails
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Payment in status 'Authorized' with authorize.net or native payment method
- SYNC NOTE VCST-5162 (backend PR#12 change #4): PaymentGatewayTransaction shape changed. 'Status' now stores short TransactionResponse enum string (e.g. 'Approved'), 'ResponseCode' stores numeric TransactionResponseCode, 'Note' carries full description + 'Transaction ID: {id}'. If Admin UI assertions on transaction Status/ResponseCode/Note labels are added in future, use this shape. No executable assertion change needed in this legacy-format case.
Steps:
1. Open order with authorized payment (authorize.net)
2. Try to capture
3. Repeat with native payment method
References: C358191 C358192; Synced: VCST-5162 (backend change #4 transaction shape note added)

Title: Capture non-authorized payment fails
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Payment NOT in status 'Authorized'
Steps:
1. Open order with non-authorized payment
2. Try to capture

Title: Refund paid payment
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Payment in status 'Paid'
Steps:
1. Open order with paid payment
2. Click 'Refund'
3. Verify refund result

Title: Refund non-paid payment fails
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Payment NOT in status 'Paid'
Steps:
1. Open order with non-paid payment
2. Try to refund

Title: Refund document management
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Refund document exists
Steps:
1. Verify refund document number template
2. Try to remove refund document
3. Verify dynamic property on refund document

Title: Payment Capture — Manual Capture
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Authorized status via manual payment method
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders
2. Open an order with payment in Authorized status
3. Open the payment document from the Documents section
4. Verify the Capture button is visible and enabled
5. Click the Capture button
6. Confirm the capture action in the dialog
7. Verify the payment status transitions

Title: Payment Refund — Full Refund
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Paid status
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders
2. Open an order with payment in Paid status
3. Open the payment document from the Documents section
4. Verify the Refund button is visible and enabled
5. Click the Refund button
6. Enter the full payment amount as the refund amount
7. Confirm the refund action
8. Verify the payment status transitions

Title: Payment Refund — Partial Refund
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Paid status with amount > $10
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders
2. Open an order with payment in Paid status
3. Open the payment document from the Documents section
4. Click the Refund button
5. Enter a partial amount (less than the total payment amount)
6. Confirm the partial refund
7. Verify the refund document and payment state
8. Verify remaining balance is correct

Title: Payment Status Transitions — Authorized to Captured to Refunded
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with manual payment method
Payment is in New status
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders and open the target order
2. Open the payment document
3. Transition payment from New to Authorized (click Authorize)
4. Verify payment is in Authorized status
5. Click Capture to transition to Paid/Captured
6. Verify payment is in Paid status
7. Click Refund and enter full amount to transition to Refunded
8. Verify final Refunded status
9. Verify each status transition is logged in the change history

Title: Payment state — Pending to Captured skipping Authorization must fail
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Pending status
Manual payment method configured
API access available via Swagger at ${BACK_URL}/docs
Steps:
1. Open Swagger UI at ${BACK_URL}/docs/index.html
2. Locate the payment status update endpoint (PUT /api/order/customerOrders)
3. Retrieve order with payment in Pending status via GET /api/order/customerOrders/{orderId}
4. Attempt to change payment status directly from Pending to Captured (skip Authorized)
5. Send the PUT request with modified payment status
6. Inspect the HTTP response status code
7. Inspect the response body for error details
8. Verify payment status remains Pending in subsequent GET request

Title: Payment state — Voided to Captured must fail
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Voided status (terminal state)
API access available via Swagger at ${BACK_URL}/docs
Steps:
1. Open Swagger UI at ${BACK_URL}/docs/index.html
2. Retrieve order with payment in Voided status via GET /api/order/customerOrders/{orderId}
3. Attempt to change payment status from Voided to Captured via PUT request
4. Send the PUT request with modified payment status
5. Inspect the HTTP response status code
6. Inspect the response body for error details
7. Verify payment status remains Voided in subsequent GET request

Title: Payment state — Refunded to Captured must fail
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Refunded status (terminal state)
API access available via Swagger at ${BACK_URL}/docs
Steps:
1. Open Swagger UI at ${BACK_URL}/docs/index.html
2. Retrieve order with payment in Refunded status via GET /api/order/customerOrders/{orderId}
3. Attempt to change payment status from Refunded to Captured via PUT request
4. Send the PUT request with modified payment status

Title: Payment state — Admin shows only valid transition buttons per state
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Orders exist with payments in various states: Pending, Authorized, Paid (Captured), Voided, Refunded
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} and open Orders module
2. Open order with payment in Pending status > Open payment document
3. Note which action buttons are visible (expected: Authorize only)
4. Open order with payment in Authorized status > Open payment document
5. Note which action buttons are visible (expected: Capture, Void only)
6. Open order with payment in Paid status > Open payment document
7. Note which action buttons are visible (expected: Refund only)
8. Open order with payment in Voided status > Open payment document
9. Verify no transition buttons are visible (terminal state)
10. Open order with payment in Refunded status > Open payment document
11. Verify no transition buttons are visible (terminal state)

Title: Refund boundary — partial refund exceeding remaining balance must fail
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment captured for $100
Partial refund of $30 already processed
Remaining refundable balance = $70
API access available via Swagger at ${BACK_URL}/docs
Steps:
1. Verify order has payment in Paid status with original amount $100
2. Verify existing partial refund of $30 is recorded
3. Attempt to issue a second refund for $80 via API (exceeds remaining $70)
4. Send the refund request
5. Inspect the HTTP response status code
6. Inspect the response body for error details
7. Verify remaining refundable balance is still $70 (unchanged)
8. Verify no refund document was created for the $80 attempt

Title: Refund boundary — voided payment cannot be refunded
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Voided status
API access available via Swagger at ${BACK_URL}/docs
Steps:
1. Retrieve order with payment in Voided status via GET /api/order/customerOrders/{orderId}
2. Attempt to issue a refund on the voided payment via API
3. Send the refund request
4. Inspect the HTTP response status code
5. Inspect the response body for error details
6. Verify payment status remains Voided
7. Verify no refund document was created

Title: Refund boundary — fully refunded payment cannot be refunded again
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment fully refunded (status = Refunded)
API access available via Swagger at ${BACK_URL}/docs
Steps:
1. Retrieve order with payment in Refunded status via GET /api/order/customerOrders/{orderId}
2. Verify refund total equals original captured amount
3. Attempt to issue an additional refund via API (any amount)
4. Send the refund request
5. Inspect the HTTP response status code
6. Inspect the response body for error details
7. Verify payment status remains Refunded
8. Verify no additional refund document was created
